items.title: Tuples
items.description: Tuples are fixed-size, immutable sequences — pick a tuple when the size and meaning of each slot are fixed.

items.title: Sets
items.description: Use a set (not a list) when you need O(1) membership tests or to deduplicate.

items.title: Dictionaries & Hash Maps
items.description: Reach for a dict when you need O(1) lookup by key instead of scanning a list.

items.title: Stacks & Queues
items.description: Lists implement a stack cheaply (append/pop on the end); use deque for a queue to keep both ends O(1).

items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: List operations are the canonical introduction to amortized analysis and the front-vs-end cost difference.
